meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'
items.summary: Czech Foreign Minister Macinka voices strong support for Trump, defends increased European defense spending and warns Iran poses a global nuclear threat.

items.title: Crash involving speeding train, minibus in Belgium leaves 4 dead including 2 children
items.summary: Four people, including two children, were killed when a passenger train hit a minibus at a crossing near Buggenhout, Belgium, during the morning rush.

items.title: Israel eliminates head of Hamas' military wing in Gaza strike
items.summary: Israeli forces killed Mohammed Odeh, the new head of Hamas' military wing, in an airstrike in northern Gaza, according to the Israel Defense Forces.

items.title: Iran and Houthi terror proxy facing Red Sea threat from pro-US African nation
items.summary: Sen. Ted Cruz advocates recognizing Somaliland as the U.S. reportedly explores military access to the breakaway state's port and airbase facilities.

items.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
items.summary: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF's president says.

items.title: Mother, boyfriend allegedly abandoned blindfolded young sons in remote forest as part of 'game': reports
items.summary: Marine Rousseau and Marc Ballabriga allegedly abandoned her two young children in a forest in Europe, blindfolding them as part of a so-called game.

items.title: ‘Designated target’ Mojtaba Khamenei to sign Trump deal in ‘unprecedented’ courier setup
items.summary: Experts say the U.S. is negotiating with an invisible counterparty as Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly remains in hiding and relies on secret couriers.

items.title: Funerals, beauty queens and bombs: The Ukrainian city that won’t let Putin win
items.summary: Mayor Sadovyi says Lviv has lost 2,000 citizens and built rehabilitation centers as the city tries to remain unbroken amid Russia's ongoing war.

items.title: World's humanitarian system buckling, 'no longer fit for purpose,' US-based researchers say
items.summary: A new Lancet report argues the global humanitarian system needs major reform as crises around the world overwhelm current aid delivery.

items.title: Iran signals ‘mass sacrifice’ in 'high stakes' Saddam-era warning amid Trump deal talks
items.summary: Expert says Iran's Pezeshkian mapped a foundational wartime symbol onto current U.S. and Israel tensions, signaling Tehran won't back down easily.

items.title: Pope Leo warns AI risks becoming tool of 'domination, exclusion and death' in new encyclical
items.summary: The pope unveils "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, warning artificial intelligence risks becoming a tool of domination unless governments set moral limits.

items.title: Canadian teen girl charged with arson after allegedly torching American baseball team's charter bus
items.summary: A Canadian teen is charged with arson after allegedly setting fire to a U.S. baseball team's charter bus during a road trip to Winnipeg, police say.

items.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
items.summary: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

items.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
items.summary: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.

items.title: 'Bibi's hair on fire': Trump-Netanyahu public 'rift' masked unified front against Iran, analyst says
items.summary: Leading defense analyst says Trump-Netanyahu tensions over Iran were a calculated strategy to blindside Tehran while building a unified front.

items.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
items.summary: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.

items.title: Ukraine’s battlefield is transforming the future of NATO
items.summary: NATO allies increasingly study Ukraine's wartime innovations in drones and defense production as the alliance prepares for its July summit in Ankara.

items.title: China deployed over 100 vessels near Taiwan in the wake of Trump-Xi summit, Taiwan security official claims
items.summary: China deployed over 100 vessels around Taiwan after the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, Taiwan National Security Council Secretary General Joseph Wu says.

items.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours
items.summary: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv issued a security alert warning of a potentially significant air attack that may occur within the next 24 hours in Ukraine.

items.title: 'A new kind of war': Inside Ukraine's hidden factories mass-producing combat drones
items.summary: Get an exclusive look inside Ukraine's hidden drone factories rapidly producing kamikaze drones and interceptor systems to counter Iranian-designed Shahed and other Russian drones.

items.title: Ebola treatment center set on fire in Congo after residents clash with authorities over victim's body
items.summary: Angry residents reportedly set fire to an Ebola treatment center in eastern Congo during a clash over retrieving the body of a suspected Ebola victim.

items.title: US arrests sister of powerful Cuban official over alleged ties to communist regime
items.summary: U.S. arrests sister of Cuba's GAESA military conglomerate leader over alleged ties to the communist regime, Secretary of State Rubio announces.

items.title: Record number of climbers summit Mount Everest from Nepali side despite overcrowding concerns
items.summary: A record 274 climbers summited Mount Everest in a single day this week, raising fresh concerns about dangerous overcrowding on the world's tallest peak.

items.title: Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines
items.summary: Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem were among 19 killed in a Philippine army firefight with suspected communist insurgents tied to the New People’s Army.

items.title: Mojtaba Khamenei using ‘bin Laden template’ to survive, learned from Abbottabad: analyst
items.summary: Analyst compares Mojtaba Khamenei's disappearance from public view to Osama bin Laden's decade of hiding, calling it a strategic milestone for the U.S.
